sourceTitle: Publish Notion pages to the web

questions.question: What does turning on the **Allow editing** toggle do for a published Notion page?

options.text: It lets anyone with the link edit the page in real‑time.
options.explanation: When the Allow editing toggle is on, anyone who can view the public link can also make changes that appear instantly.

options.text: It makes the page searchable by Google.
options.explanation: Search engine indexing is controlled by a separate toggle, not Allow editing.

options.text: It automatically creates a duplicate copy of the page in each visitor’s workspace.
options.explanation: Duplicating as a template is a different option; Allow editing does not copy the page.

options.text: It locks the page so no one can view it.
options.explanation: Allow editing does the opposite of locking; it grants editing rights.

questions.question: Which setting lets you set an expiration date for a published Notion page’s URL?

options.text: Link expires
options.explanation: The “Link expires” option is specifically designed to add an expiration date to the public link.

options.text: Allow editing
options.explanation: Allow editing controls who can edit, not when the link stops working.

options.text: Search engine indexing
options.explanation: Search engine indexing determines if search engines can index the page, not its lifespan.

options.text: Allow duplicate as template
options.explanation: That option controls whether others can copy the page, not its expiration.

questions.question: If you want anyone to be able to copy your published page into their own Notion workspace, which option must be enabled?

options.text: Allow duplicate as template
options.explanation: Enabling “Allow duplicate as template” permits visitors to duplicate the page into their own workspace.

options.text: Allow editing
options.explanation: Allow editing lets visitors edit the published page, but not duplicate it as a template.

options.text: Link expires
options.explanation: Link expiration only limits how long the link works; it does not affect duplication.

options.text: Search engine indexing
options.explanation: Search engine indexing controls visibility to search engines, not duplication rights.

questions.question: Turning on which feature allows your published Notion page to appear in Google search results?

options.text: Search engine indexing
options.explanation: When search engine indexing is enabled, search engines like Google can crawl and index the page.

options.text: Allow editing
options.explanation: Allow editing only grants edit rights; it does not affect search engine visibility.

options.text: Allow duplicate as template
options.explanation: Duplicating as a template is unrelated to search engine indexing.

options.text: Link expires
options.explanation: Link expiration determines how long the link remains active, not whether it can be indexed.

questions.question: Where can you stop a page from being publicly available?

options.text: In the Publish tab, by clicking **Unpublish**
options.explanation: The guide states you can unpublish your page at any time from the Publish tab.

options.text: In the Share tab, by turning off **Allow editing**
options.explanation: Turning off Allow editing only removes edit rights; it does not unpublish the page.

options.text: By deleting the page entirely from your workspace
options.explanation: Deleting the page removes it, but the specific unpublish action is performed in the Publish tab.

options.text: By setting a **Link expires** date in the past
options.explanation: An expired link stops working, but the page remains published; unpublishing is a distinct action.

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes the difference between the **Share tab** and the **Publish tab**?

options.text: The Share tab controls how the page is shared within your Notion workspace, while the Publish tab manages the page’s public web presence and its publishing settings.
options.explanation: The guide notes the Share tab is for internal sharing decisions, whereas the Publish tab handles public publishing options such as unpublishing, link expiration, and indexing.

options.text: Both tabs perform the same functions; they are just duplicated for convenience.
options.explanation: They serve distinct purposes; they are not duplicates.

options.text: The Share tab lets you edit the page’s content, while the Publish tab only lets you view analytics.
options.explanation: Editing is done on the page itself; the Share tab does not provide editing capabilities, nor does the Publish tab show analytics.

options.text: The Publish tab is only visible after you enable **Allow editing** in the Share tab.
options.explanation: Publishing a page does not depend on the Allow editing toggle in the Share tab; the Publish tab is always available for a page you wish to make public.

questions.question: Which of the following is **NOT** mentioned as a typical use case for publishing a Notion page?

options.text: Streaming live video
options.explanation: The article lists handbooks, resumes, knowledge bases, recipes, business websites, and travel blogs, but not video streaming.

options.text: Employee handbooks
options.explanation: Employee handbooks are explicitly mentioned as a use case.

options.text: Travel blogs
options.explanation: Travel blogs are listed among the examples.

options.text: Recipes
options.explanation: Recipes are also highlighted as a possible published page type.

questions.question: What does the **Start with a template** section encourage users to do?

options.text: Browse over 10,000 templates in the Notion template gallery
options.explanation: The section explicitly invites users to explore the template gallery, which contains more than 10,000 templates.

options.text: Immediately publish the current page without any customization
options.explanation: While you can publish any page, the section specifically mentions browsing templates first.

options.text: Set an expiration date for the public link
options.explanation: Link expiration is a separate setting; it isn’t the focus of the template section.

options.text: Enable search engine indexing for the page
options.explanation: Search engine indexing is another toggle, unrelated to the template recommendation.
